file_name: SmartRxSystemsInc_20180914_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11351705_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: The MDO will be payable within ninety (90) days from the date of the transaction (First Month payable by the end of ninety (90) days from the end of the month).
perturbation.changed_text: The MDO will be payable within thirty (30) days from the date of the transaction (First Month payable by the end of thirty (30) days from the end of the month).
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment deadline for the Master Distributor Override (MDO) from ninety (90) days to thirty (30) days creates an in-text contradiction. This conflicts with section 4.1 (ii), which states the payment timeline is 90 days. The change introduces ambiguity about when the payment is due, potentially leading to disputes and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment to the Distributor for the sale of the Products to Customers shall be as follows: (i) Company will pay Distributor (or its Assignee) Ten Percent (10%) of the Master Distributor Override generated by each Customer Kiosk Location. (Master Distributor Override ("MDO") shall mean Total Revenue per Customer Kiosk Location collected by SRXS from third party payors and patient cash payments). This 10% of the total revenue generated is payable only if the EBITDA is a positive EBITDA and exceeds the 10% of the revenue. In cases of a positive EBITDA but shortage of funds compared to the 10% of the revenue, Distributor will take less than 10%. If the EBITDA is negative, no payments will be made to Distributor. Additionally, it will be the sole responsibility of Distributor to make a full disclosure of the 10% MDO to all its clients. (ii) The MDO will be payable within thirty (30) days from the date of the transaction (First Month payable by the end of thirty (30) days from the end of the month). (iii) For each Customer Kiosk contracted and implemented by Distributor, Company shall also pay a management fee of 40% of Net Income (Net Income for this purpose is defined as EBITDA less percentage paid to the client. The balance is then split 40%/60% to A3 Development Group, LLC and Smart RX Systems, Inc. respectively). Net Income is defined as total Customer revenue collections less all incurred expenses, and is the distributable cash generated by each Customer location. These payments will also follow the payment policy outlined in 4.1 above. (iv) The Payment(s) to Distributor or its Assignee, as set forth herein, shall be further set forth in each Class A Series Agreement, along with the respective ownership interest for both the Company and the Distributor (or its Assignee) for each respective Kiosk Location which shall have its own separate Series, as defined by the Texas Business Organizations Code for Series Limited Liability Companies, and as set forth in the Company's Operating Agreement. (v) The Payments set forth in Section 4.1(i) and 4.1(iii) above will reflect the following:
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline for the MDO from 90 days to 30 days. This directly contradicts section 4.1 (ii) which explicitly states "The MDO will be payable within thirty (30) days from the date of the transaction (First Month payable by the end of thirty (30) days from the end of the month)." as well. The original text was 90 days but the Changed Text specifies 30 days. This creates a strong inconsistency as to when the MDO payment is due, leading to a dispute.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall: (i) pay all valid outstanding and final invoices issued by Company within thirty (30) days after termination is effective;
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall: (i) pay all valid outstanding and final invoices issued by Company within sixty (60) days after termination is effective;
perturbation.explanation: Changing the invoice payment deadline after termination from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days introduces an in-text contradiction in section 13.1 (i). The original text specifies 30 days for payment of outstanding invoices after termination. This creates confusion and uncertainty about the payment deadline upon termination, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor Obligations. Distributor shall: (i) pay all valid outstanding and final invoices issued by Company within sixty (60) days after termination is effective; (ii) immediately cease all distribution, duplication, and representation of the Products and marketing literature; (iii) immediately cease all use of any Company trademarks, and not there after use any mark which is confusingly similar to any trademark associated with any Product; (iv) immediately cease all representation of an existing relationship with Company; and (v) within thirty (30) days deliver to Company copies of all customer records (electronic, paper or otherwise) related to the Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days, directly conflicting with the original stated in the Contradicted Text which mentions "Distributor shall: (i) pay all valid outstanding and final invoices issued by Company within sixty (60) days after termination is effective;". This falls under the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, as the 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines'. This creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor agrees to provide Company, within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar quarter, with a report depicting sales and sales leads, including volume projections.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor agrees to provide Company, within sixty (60) days after the end of each calendar quarter, with a report depicting sales and sales leads, including volume projections.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the deadline for quarterly reports from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days after the end of the calendar quarter creates an in-text contradiction. This contradiction affects Section 4.6, causing confusion about the correct reporting timeframe. This could lead to disputes over compliance and reporting obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Quarterly Reports, Distributor agrees to provide Company, within sixty (60) days after the end of each calendar quarter, with a report depicting sales and sales leads, including volume projections. Distributor shall commence providing Company such reports within thirty (30) days following the end of the calendar quarter in which this Agreement is executed.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 30-day reporting period, while the changed text extends it to 60 days. The contradicted text states 'Distributor shall commence providing Company such reports within thirty (30) days following the end of the calendar quarter'. The change to 60 days directly conflicts with the existing 30-day requirement, creating a clear inconsistency regarding the reporting timeframe, adhering to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
